result:
The task is complete. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.calr`, the build is clean, and all behavioral checks pass, including full round-trip verification.

What I added to the `CsvRow` module (keeping `NeedsQuoting` untouched):

- **`EscapeField`** — calls `NeedsQuoting`; if quoting is needed it doubles internal quotes via `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")` and wraps the result in double-quotes, otherwise returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** — walks the list with a while loop, escaping each field and inserting commas between them; an empty list yields the empty string. Takes `List<str>` (the initial `[str]` spelling generated a `string[]`, which broke both `§CNT` and the pinned `List<string>` surface, so I switched to the inline generic type syntax).
- **`SplitRow`** — a character-scanning parser with `mut` effect (it builds the result list with `§PUSH`): a field starting with `"` consumes until the un-doubled closing quote, turning `""` into one literal quote and treating commas/CR/LF inside quotes as content; unquoted fields end at the next comma or end of line; a final push handles both the empty line (one empty field) and trailing commas (trailing empty field).

Verification: it took 2 of the 10 build cycles, and I exercised the compiled assembly with a throwaway console project in `/tmp` (removed afterward — no test files in the source tree). Checks covered every escaping rule from the spec, `JoinRow([]) == ""`, the empty-line and trailing-comma parse rules, and `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` round-trips over adversarial inputs like `a"`, `""`, `",“`, and embedded CR/LF — all passed.
